tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal odor, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, proteinuria) may indicate possible urinary tract pathology or dehydration. Elevated chloride and low HbA1c are noted. No evidence of urinary infection (negative nitrite/leukocyte esterase). Recommend further renal evaluation and clinical correlation for possible chronic kidney disease and underlying causes of abnormal urine findings.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
